title: Stock News Analyst Brief — User Message Template
description: User message template for the Stock News Analyst Brief workflow. Injects raw market data and current date, instructs the model to search for news from the last 24 hours only.

title: Stock News Analyst — Brief
description: Lightweight version of Stock News Analyst. Returns importance score and a single-sentence Polish description. Designed for high-volume or space-constrained workflows.

title: Stock News Analyst — User Message Template
description: User message template for the Stock News Analyst workflow. Injects raw market data and current date, then instructs the model to search for fresh news and return an importance score with description.

title: Stock News Analyst
description: Analyzes stock news and company data, returning a structured JSON with ticker symbol, importance score (0–10), and a concise Polish-language explanation of price movement.

title: Conventional Commit Message Generator
description: Generates structured Conventional Commits messages from changed files in context. Produces subject + body explaining why and how the change was made — useful for both humans and AI agents reading history.

title: CodeMentor
description: Personal programming tutor with Socratic teaching method, hint escalation system and Google Sheets progress tracking.

additional_data.description: Short professional summary – who you are, years of experience, industry, strengths.
What you want to achieve with this course. Specific, measurable goal.
Desired competence level. Options: beginner / intermediate / advanced / interview-ready
Current learning topic. Update more frequently than the rest of the profile.

title: Project Ideas Generator
description: Generates 4 unique project ideas tailored to a personal tech toolkit — 2 light projects, 1 flagship MVP concept, and 1 pure programming productivity tool. Balances software-first thinking with practical usefulness.

additional_data.description: Today's date. Used to provide seasonal or time-aware project suggestions.

title: Thought to Action Plan
description: Converts raw incoming thoughts into a structured JSON action plan. Classifies input as accepted or rejected, generates goal, step-by-step tasks with time estimates, and an engineer comment. Strict data processor — no conversational output.

title: Compose Component Creator
description: Create idiomatic, testable Jetpack Compose components following Material 3 best practices, with multi-variant Previews, test tags, and behavioral tests using ComposeTestRule. Covers creating components from UiState sealed classes, screenshots, Figma exports, or prose descriptions.
trigger_description: Use when creating, refactoring, or reviewing any @Composable component — list rows, cards, buttons, input fields, small composed widgets, or screen skeletons. Also trigger when the user provides a screen mockup, Figma export, Claude Design file, or phone screenshot and asks to turn it into Compose code; when they mention a sealed UiState class that a component should render; when .kt files with @Composable annotations are involved; or when the user mentions Compose + testTag, semantics, mergeDescendants, PreviewParameter, or ComposeTestRule. Do NOT use for full-app architecture (ViewModels, navigation graphs, DI modules).

title: Course Planner
description: Generates a fully interactive HTML course page from a topic, experience level, and learning goal. Output includes collapsible modules with lesson cards, 3-state status tracking (not started / in progress / done), per-lesson notes persisted in localStorage, copyable lesson and test prompts with role context injected, a pace calculator slider, and confetti celebrations on lesson/module/course completion. Course content is in Polish; technical names and programming terms stay in English.
trigger_description: Use whenever the user wants to learn something new, master a technology, or asks for a course plan, curriculum, or study schedule — even phrased casually like 'jak się nauczyć X', 'zrób mi plan kursu', 'chcę opanować Y', or 'give me a study plan for...'. Also trigger when the user describes a desired proficiency level ('chcę móc swobodnie pracować z Room') or wants to link a topic to existing skills or projects. Do NOT use for one-off explanations or Q&A — only when the deliverable is a structured multi-module curriculum.

title: Customaize Agent: Prompt Engineering
description: Advanced prompt engineering skill covering few-shot learning, chain-of-thought, template systems, and system prompt design. Includes agent-specific best practices (context window management, freedom levels) and persuasion principles (Authority, Commitment, Social Proof, Unity) backed by empirical research. Use when writing commands, hooks, skills for Claude Code agents, or prompts for sub-agents and any other LLM interaction.
trigger_description: Use when writing commands, hooks, or skills for Claude Code agents, prompts for sub-agents, or any other LLM interaction — including optimizing existing prompts, improving LLM output reliability, or designing production prompt templates.

title: Developer Growth Analysis
description: Analyzes recent Claude Code chat history (last 24-48 hours) to identify coding patterns, recurring challenges, and skill gaps. Generates a prioritized growth report with evidence-based improvement areas, curated HackerNews learning resources, and automatically delivers it to Slack DMs.
trigger_description: Use when the user wants to understand their development patterns from recent work, identify technical gaps, get curated learning resources tailored to their actual work, or receive a structured growth report without waiting for a code review.

title: Docker Compose
description: Skill for writing production-quality Docker Compose setups. Enforces pinned image versions, named volumes and networks, .env-based secrets, inter-service networking, custom runner Dockerfiles (e.g. n8n + Python), startup/stop scripts for multi-service stacks, and ARM64 / Raspberry Pi 5 compatibility checks.
trigger_description: Use when writing or reviewing docker-compose.yml files, creating multi-service container stacks, configuring inter-service networking, adding custom Dockerfiles or sidecar runners, writing startup/stop scripts, or setting up Docker projects intended to run on Raspberry Pi 5 (ARM64).

title: Kotlin TDD
description: Write, review, and refactor idiomatic Kotlin code using a strict TDD workflow (red → green → refactor) with JUnit 5, MockK, and Turbine. Covers writing new code test-first, structured code review, and safe step-by-step refactoring.
trigger_description: Use when writing new Kotlin classes, functions, or modules; reviewing or refactoring existing Kotlin code; working with coroutines, Flow, sealed classes, or data classes; or when the user references .kt files, JUnit 5, MockK, or Turbine.

title: Prompt Vault JSON Generator
description: Generates a prompt-vault-compatible JSON metadata file that pairs with any .md prompt or .skill file, enabling indexing by generate_embeddings.py. Extracts and infers all required fields (title, category, tags, placeholders, etc.) from the source file's frontmatter and body.
trigger_description: Use whenever the user asks to create a JSON for a prompt or skill, wants to add something to prompt-vault, or drops a file without explanation expecting a JSON output. Trigger on phrases like "napisz jsona", "zrób json", "dodaj do prompt-vault". Also trigger when the user pastes a SKILL.md frontmatter or prompt file and no JSON exists yet.

title: Trip Ideas Generator
description: Generates 4 unique trip proposals based on base city and current date — from a city date to a long vacation. Avoids tourist traps, focuses on atmospheric and off-the-beaten-path places.

additional_data.description: Today's date in DD.MM.YYYY format. Used to determine current season and plan ahead.
Base city for the trip proposals. Used as a starting point for all 4 trip types.
